nodes.label: Core Design Pillars: Simple mechanic, Atmosphere over volume, Environmental narrative, Ship complete
nodes.norm_label: core design pillars: simple mechanic, atmosphere over volume, environmental narrative, ship complete

nodes.label: Rationale: Atmosphere over content volume — 3 hand-crafted cases per day preferred over 10 sloppy ones
nodes.norm_label: rationale: atmosphere over content volume — 3 hand-crafted cases per day preferred over 10 sloppy ones

nodes.label: Rationale: Narrative told through background changes and notifications — no cutscenes or dialogue trees
nodes.norm_label: rationale: narrative told through background changes and notifications — no cutscenes or dialogue trees

properties.description: A green cartoon humanoid robot or alien character with red spiky hair, red hands, and red feet. Simple, stylized low-fidelity pixel-art-adjacent illustration style. Upright bipedal stance.
Design concept for a factory worker character fitting the cyberpunk aesthetic — a mechanized or alien humanoid that could represent the 'machine' identity central to the game's theme.
properties.game_relevance: You're Just a Machine — the character visually embodies the game's core theme of being a non-human worker in a dehumanizing system

nodes.label: Cyberpunk Visual Aesthetic — Stylized Low-Fi Art

properties.description: The art style uses a deliberately simple, hand-drawn cartoon approach consistent with a game jam aesthetic. The robotic/alien green figure with red accents evokes a retro-cyberpunk feel.
properties.style_notes: low-fidelity, expressive linework, vibrant contrasting colors

nodes.norm_label: cyberpunk visual aesthetic — stylized low-fi art

nodes.label: You're Just a Machine — Phaser 3 Game Jam Project

properties.description: The game project this artwork belongs to. A cyberpunk factory worker game built with Phaser 3 for a game jam. The player character is described as 'just a machine', aligning with the robotic character depicted.

nodes.label: You're Just a Machine - Cyberpunk Factory Worker Game

nodes.label: Main Gameplay Screen
nodes.description: Primary in-game view showing the factory floor where the player evaluates items on a conveyor belt using a machine terminal

nodes.label: Machine Terminal
nodes.description: Large industrial machine on the left side of the screen with a green glowing pixel display/screen, representing the worker's evaluation station

nodes.label: Decision Legend / Color-Code Panel
nodes.description: UI legend panel attached to the machine terminal listing three decision outcomes: red dot = SCRAP, yellow dot = REPAIR, green dot = APPROVE

nodes.label: SCRAP Decision Option
nodes.description: Red indicator in the decision legend representing the player choice to scrap/discard an item on the conveyor belt

nodes.label: REPAIR Decision Option
nodes.description: Yellow indicator in the decision legend representing the player choice to send an item for repair

nodes.label: APPROVE Decision Option
nodes.description: Green indicator in the decision legend representing the player choice to approve and pass an item

nodes.label: Conveyor Belt
nodes.description: Horizontal conveyor belt running along the bottom of the screen with circular rollers, transporting boxes for player evaluation

nodes.label: Conveyor Belt Boxes
nodes.description: Multiple brown/orange labeled 'Box' items on the conveyor belt, representing items the player must evaluate (scrap/repair/approve)

nodes.label: Hanging Industrial Lamp
nodes.description: Pixel-art hanging light fixture suspended from the factory ceiling, providing atmospheric industrial lighting to the scene

nodes.label: Floating Pixel Particles / Sprite
nodes.description: Small scattered white pixel dots floating in the center of the screen — possibly sparks, debris, dust, or a stylized character/entity sprite

nodes.label: Factory Background / Environment
nodes.description: Dark brown and grey industrial factory interior environment, establishing the dystopian cyberpunk factory aesthetic of the game

nodes.label: Pixel Art Visual Style
nodes.description: Consistent low-resolution pixel art aesthetic used throughout the scene, typical of game jam projects and retro-styled indie games

nodes.label: Core Gameplay Loop — Item Evaluation
nodes.description: The central mechanic shown: items arrive on the conveyor belt and the player must decide to SCRAP, REPAIR, or APPROVE each one, referencing the machine terminal's legend

nodes.label: Inspect View UI Screen
nodes.description: The main inspection view screen of the game 'You're Just a Machine', where the player examines a factory machine/item and decides its fate.

nodes.label: Machine Subject (Conveyor Item)
nodes.description: The pixel-art object being inspected, shown on the left side. Appears to be a smiley-face character/machine on a conveyor belt — the factory item the player must evaluate.

nodes.label: Decision Panel (Scrap / Repair / Approve)
nodes.description: Top-right UI panel listing three decision options: SCRAP, REPAIR, APPROVE, each paired with a colored indicator dot (red, yellow, teal/green respectively). The player selects one verdict for the inspected machine.

nodes.label: Scrap Option
nodes.description: Decision option to scrap the machine; associated with a red indicator dot, implying rejection/destruction.

nodes.label: Repair Option
nodes.description: Decision option to repair the machine; associated with a yellow indicator dot, implying a fixable/intermediate state.

nodes.label: Approve Option
nodes.description: Decision option to approve the machine; associated with a teal/green indicator dot, implying passing quality control.

nodes.label: Tool Bar Panel
nodes.description: Bottom-right UI panel labeled 'Tool Bar', displaying available tools: a hammer icon and a spray/gun-type tool icon. Used during the repair/inspection phase to interact with the machine.

nodes.label: Hammer Tool
nodes.description: Pixel-art hammer icon shown in the Tool Bar panel; likely used to repair physical damage on machines.

nodes.label: Spray / Gun Tool
nodes.description: Pixel-art spray or gun-type tool shown in the Tool Bar panel; likely used to apply paint, lubricant, or another repair action on machines.

nodes.label: Conveyor Belt Background
nodes.description: The grey tiled/paneled background on the left side of the screen, representing the factory conveyor environment where machines are presented for inspection.

nodes.label: Pixel Art Visual Style
nodes.description: The overall visual design language: low-resolution pixel art with a dark/grey cyberpunk industrial palette, consistent across all UI panels and game objects.

nodes.label: Color-Coded Decision Indicators
nodes.description: Design pattern using red/yellow/green dots next to decision options (Scrap/Repair/Approve) to provide instant visual feedback about severity or outcome of each choice.

nodes.label: Hanging Light Bulb (warm glow, suspended from ceiling)
nodes.norm_label: hanging light bulb (warm glow, suspended from ceiling)

nodes.label: Factory Control Buttons (red and green switches)
nodes.norm_label: factory control buttons (red and green switches)

nodes.label: Sentry Frame Sprite
nodes.description: Pixel art sprite of a humanoid robot sentry. Features a dark brown/maroon blocky body, a red rectangular torso panel, a small square head with a red arc visor/sensor, stubby arms, and an oval grey shadow base. Styled as a factory guard or security machine.

nodes.label: Sentry Robot
nodes.description: In-game entity: a sentry/guard robot within the cyberpunk factory setting of 'You're Just a Machine'. Acts as a machine subject for player inspection.

nodes.label: Pixel Art Style
nodes.description: Visual style attribute: low-resolution pixel art aesthetic consistent with the game jam project's retro-cyberpunk aesthetic.

nodes.label: Red Arc Visor/Sensor
nodes.description: Visual component on the sentry's head: a small red arc shape suggesting a scanning visor or optical sensor, implying surveillance or threat-detection capability.

nodes.label: Cyberpunk Factory Context
nodes.description: Game world context: the sentry exists within a dystopian cyberpunk factory environment as part of the game 'You're Just a Machine', a Phaser 3 game jam project.

nodes.label: Assembler Alpha Machine Sprite
nodes.description: Flat pixel-art style robot sprite with dark navy/charcoal body, cyan eye panels, three horizontal cyan display bars on torso, a small circular sensor, square shoulder protrusions, and a gray elliptical shadow base. Represents an assembler machine unit in the game.

nodes.label: Assembler Alpha (Game Entity)
nodes.description: An assembler machine entity in the cyberpunk factory game 'You're Just a Machine'. Likely performs assembly tasks on the factory floor as part of the automated production pipeline.

nodes.label: You're Just a Machine (Game)
nodes.description: A Phaser 3 cyberpunk factory worker game jam project. Player navigates as a factory worker among automated machines.

nodes.label: Robot / Industrial Machine Visual Design
nodes.description: Visual design concept: a friendly-looking blocky humanoid robot representing industrial automation. Features a rectangular head with two small cyan eyes, torso with three horizontal cyan bars (conveyor/display panels), circular sensor node, and squared shoulder panels.

nodes.label: Cyan Display Bars (Visual Element)
nodes.description: Three horizontal cyan-colored bars on the assembler's torso, likely representing a conveyor belt, status display, or assembly interface. Gives the machine a high-tech, cyberpunk aesthetic.

nodes.label: Machine Sprite (Asset Category)
nodes.description: Asset category for machine sprites used in the cyberpunk factory game. Located in assets/machines/sprites/.
